hub_subtitle: Business English for HR leadership in international meetings

lesson.menu_title: Every Day vs. Right Now — Mastering the Contrast That Confuses Everyone
lesson.menu_desc: Separating routines from live action in HR meetings: present simple vs present continuous -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 5 -- Every Day vs. Right Now -- Present Simple vs Present Continuous
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Murielle Xavier | Aula 5 -- Every Day vs. Right Now

listenings.text: Hi Murielle, it's Liam. Let me tell you how a normal Monday works on my side, so we can plan our calls. Usually, I start the day at eight, and I check the overnight reports before anyone arrives. Every Monday, I run our regional stand-up meeting, and I always share the numbers from the week before. I am in charge of the Australian recruitment budget, so people often escalate difficult cases to me. But this week is a little different. Right now, I am covering for my director, who is on leave, so I am attending three extra meetings a day. I am also dealing with a recurring problem in our payroll system. Normally I prefer email, but at the moment I am calling people directly because it is faster. I notice that we work better when we talk, so let's keep these calls. Talk soon.

Good morning, Murielle. This is Diana from the London office. I want to give you some context before our meeting. Normally, our hiring process involves four steps, and every candidate meets three managers. We usually assign each new role to one recruiter, and that recruiter follows up with the candidate every week. That is our standard routine. However, this month we are testing a new, shorter process, so right now we are running only two interview rounds. I am personally following up with the candidates this week because we are still learning how the new system works. It is a one-off change for now, not a permanent one. I prefer the shorter process so far, but I want your opinion. Please look at the agenda I am sending, and let's discuss it tomorrow. Thank you.
